summary: Iteration 6 re-test CONFIRMS both iteration_5 fixes. Backend test_iteration5.py (20/20 PASS in isolation, including the two previously-failing tests test_refresh_dies_after_epoch_bump and test_patient_create_audit_metadata_has_no_phi). Added a new targeted test file test_iteration6_refresh.py (6/6 PASS) covering POST /api/auth/refresh for all four roles (admin/doctor/staff/patient) plus two epoch-bump 401 scenarios (admin disable+re-enable; admin role patch). Code review confirms: services/identity/router.py::refresh now decodes the refresh cookie, enforces payload.epoch == user.session_epoch (returns 401 on mismatch), and calls create_access_token with 5 args (sub, email, role, epoch, sst) reusing the original session_started_at claim. services/patient/router.py::create_patient audit now writes metadata={} with phi_accessed=False — no PHI leaks. No regressions observed: login for all 4 roles still returns mfa_policy_required on the user payload, admin MFA reset + require-MFA endpoints still 200/403 by role, password-reset request→confirm single-use enforced, audit CSV export admin-only still 200 with header+rows. Frontend was not retested (explicitly skipped per request; was 100% green in iteration_5 with no frontend code changes).
critical_code_review_comments: services/identity/router.py::refresh correctly reuses the refresh token's original sst (session_started_at) claim when issuing the new access token — this preserves stable session_started_at across refreshes, which is the intended behaviour and matches login/mfa_challenge/change_password sites. Good.

Suggestion (non-blocking): extract a small helper _issue_access_cookie(response, user, sst) to DRY up the five token-issuing sites (login, mfa_challenge, refresh, change_password, mfa_verify/disable, register). Same recommendation from iteration_5 stands — future auth hardening PRs are more likely to stay consistent with a single helper.

services/patient/router.py::create_patient metadata={} is the safest possible value but it also drops useful non-PHI signals (e.g. the caller's intent, record counts, gender enum). If product wants richer audit telemetry later, adopt a whitelist: AUDIT_METADATA_ALLOWED = {'gender','provider_id','count','fields'} and reject anything else at write time. Whitelist > blacklist for PHI safety. Not a bug — forward-looking note.

services/patient/router.py::update_patient, delete_patient, records endpoints should be spot-checked with the same PHI-metadata-scan approach. iteration_5 test only scans patient.created metadata; adding the same needle-based PHI scan to .updated and .records.created would close the audit-PHI story end-to-end. Not a regression, coverage gap only.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6_refresh.py (NEW — 6 tests, all PASS: 4 per-role refresh success + disable/enable epoch-bump 401 + role-patch epoch-bump 401)

success_rate.backend: 100% (20/20 iteration_5 re-run + 6/6 new iteration_6 refresh tests)
success_rate.frontend: not retested (skipped per request; was 100% green in iteration_5)

test_credentials: admin/doctor/staff/patient @ccms.app with COMPLIANCECLINIC1 suffix — all 4 seed creds verified working.
seed_data_creation: test_iteration6_refresh.py creates 2 throwaway TEST_it6_*@ccms.app staff users per run, both left disabled at end (safe). test_iteration5.py creates ~6-8 TEST_it5_*@ccms.app users per run (unchanged). No seed user mutations.
redis_note: Redis unavailable in this pod (connection refused 127.0.0.1:6379) — as stated in request, expected. Redis-dependent tests in test_iteration4.py and test_perf_cache_rate.py fail for this reason only; NOT a regression from the two fixes. Ignored per request instructions.
rate_limit_note: When running test_iteration5.py and test_iteration6_refresh.py back-to-back without delay, the public URL's login rate-limiter can return 429 on a few tests (~3/26). Running each file in isolation reproduces 100% green. This is a test-harness timing artifact, not a backend bug — the rate-limit is a security feature.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both iteration_5 bugs are confirmed fixed. The authoritative rerun command is: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://chiro-checkout-flow.preview.emergentagent.com MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5.py /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6_refresh.py -v. Run these two files in separate invocations with ~60s gap if hitting 429. test_iteration4.py and test_perf_cache_rate.py require Redis and will fail in this pod — ignore those. Frontend did not change and was 100% green in iteration_5; no need to retest unless new frontend code lands.
rca_of_issue: Both bugs (refresh 500 + audit PHI leak) are FIXED and verified — see iteration_5 report for RCA. No new bugs found in this iteration.
